speaker: patient
text: Doctor, I've noticed swelling in my ankles and lower legs over the past few days—especially worse by evening.

speaker: doctor
text: Thank you for sharing that. Given your history, I'm going to start you on Lisinopril 20mg once daily to help manage both blood pressure and kidney protection.

speaker: doctor
text: Your heart rate today is 67 beats per minute—within normal range.

speaker: doctor
text: I’m also seeing your systolic blood pressure at 91 mmHg—on the lower side, especially with the edema, so we’ll monitor closely.

speaker: doctor
text: You have a low-grade fever—38.5 °C—which could suggest an underlying inflammatory or infectious process we’ll need to investigate further.

speaker: doctor
text: Based on your labs and imaging, the primary diagnosis is Chronic Kidney Disease Stage 3. We’ll focus on slowing progression and managing complications.

speaker: patient
text: That makes sense—I’ve been feeling more fatigued lately, and my urine seems foamy sometimes.

speaker: doctor
text: Those are consistent with CKD. Let’s also check your blood glucose—we’ll want to rule out diabetes as a contributing factor.

speaker: doctor
text: Your fasting blood glucose is 6.6 mmol/L—elevated but not yet in the diabetic range. We’ll repeat this and consider HbA1c.

speaker: patient
text: I’ve been trying to cut back on salt, but the swelling hasn’t improved much—even with the Lisinopril.

speaker: doctor
text: That’s important to note. Edema despite ACE inhibition may point to volume overload or possible medication limitation due to renal function.

speaker: patient
text: I did take my temperature this morning—it was normal—but yesterday it spiked again after my afternoon walk.

speaker: doctor
text: We’ll keep tracking that fever. Let’s reassess vital signs now—your heart rate has increased since earlier.

speaker: doctor
text: Yes—your current heart rate is 87 bpm, up from 67 earlier today. That could reflect dehydration, fever response, or early cardiac strain given the edema.

speaker: patient
text: I’ve also been feeling lightheaded when standing up quickly—could that be related?

speaker: doctor
text: Possibly—orthostatic symptoms plus low BP and rising HR raise concern for volume status or autonomic involvement. We’ll adjust management accordingly.

speaker: patient
text: My glucose reading this morning was the same as the one you saw earlier—6.6 mmol/L.

speaker: doctor
text: Thanks—that’s consistent with the result from earlier today. We’ll watch for trends and consider whether insulin resistance is playing a role.

speaker: doctor
text: Good news—your temperature has come down to 37.3 °C now, so the fever appears to be resolving.

speaker: doctor
text: Given your persistent edema, borderline low BP, and rising heart rate, I’m going to switch you from Lisinopril to Amlodipine 10mg once daily—it’s safer in advanced CKD and better for volume-sensitive hypertension.
